title: Frequently Asked Questions
subtitle: Everything you need to know about how CodeCroupier works.

items.question: What is C-Chip ($CCHIP)?
items.answer: C-Chip is the utility token used to place bets and receive payouts inside CodeCroupier. It's held entirely in your own wallet, never on our servers.

items.question: How are roulette results determined?
items.answer: Results are produced by on-chain contract logic rather than a hidden server, so every spin can be independently verified after the fact by anyone.

items.question: Does CodeCroupier custody my funds?
items.answer: No. CodeCroupier is non-custodial — your balance lives in your connected wallet, and you approve every transaction yourself.

items.question: Is there a guaranteed return?
items.answer: No. CodeCroupier is a game of chance, not an investment product. Outcomes are variable and you should only ever bet what you can afford to lose.

items.question: What security guarantees do I have?
items.answer: The contract code is published and auditable. Because we never hold custody of your tokens, a compromise of our website cannot move funds out of your wallet without your explicit signature.

items.question: How do I create a CodeCroupier account?
items.answer: Connect a Web3 wallet like MetaMask from the homepage — there's no separate username or password to manage.

items.question: Which networks are supported?
items.answer: CodeCroupier currently supports EVM-compatible networks. Check the dashboard for the active network before betting.

items.question: Can I track my bet history?
items.answer: Yes. Every bet, spin, and settlement is logged on-chain and visible in your dashboard's transaction history.
